hawkish_phrases: Monetary policy will need to remain restrictive for sufficiently long
Key indicators of inflation persistence have continued to moderate, although they remain elevated
services inflation was 5.7% in May...somewhat higher than projected in the May Report
more evidence of diminishing inflation persistence was needed before reducing the degree of monetary policy restrictiveness
risks to the outlook for activity were skewed to the upside
dovish_phrases: Twelve-month CPI inflation fell to 2.0% in May
Indicators of short-term inflation expectations have also continued to moderate, particularly for households
The collective steer from a range of indicators of aggregate pay growth has continued to ease
For these members, the policy decision at this meeting was finely balanced
Two members preferred to reduce Bank Rate by 0.25 percentage points
summary: Bank Rate held at 5.25% by a 7-2 vote with two members dissenting for a 25bp cut, maintaining restrictive policy despite inflation reaching target, with some members viewing the decision as finely balanced while others sought more evidence of diminishing inflation persistence.
